authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC Franchise Rule is the federal baseline for franchise definition, disclosure timing, disclosure item content, prohibited conduct, and document-furnishing requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC guidance clarifying recordkeeping, annual and quarterly update cadence, receipt retention, electronic disclosure conditions, and financial performance representation handling.
used_for: annual and quarterly FDD update cadence

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA guidance is the core registration-state workflow reference for uniform franchise filing forms, state cover pages, effective dates, receipt handling, and state-law differences layered on top of the FTC rule.
used_for: registration-state forms and filing packet structure
state cover page and effective-date handling

authoritative_sources.title: Coordinated Review
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NASAA workflow reference for how multi-state franchise offering registration can be streamlined and reviewed under uniform standards.

authoritative_sources.title: Financial Performance Representation Commentary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's commentary gives authoritative registration-state guidance on reasonable basis, written substantiation, admonitions, projections, and disclaimer limits for Item 19 financial performance representations.

authoritative_sources.title: About the Franchise Investment Law
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DFPI is the official California regulator explaining registration-before-offer requirements and the delivery of registration disclosure documents and final franchise agreements before sale.

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions on How to File a Complete Franchise Application
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California instructions specify the exact evidence bundle and deficient-application triggers for registration and renewal packets, including clean and marked FDDs, audited financials, seller disclosures, and consents.
used_for: seller, financial, and consent evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: California Code, BPC 20020
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official statutory text establishes a concrete relationship-law example for good-cause, notice, and cure boundaries that materially constrain franchise compliance handling.
used_for: termination and cure notice boundary examples

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 37301:2021 Compliance management systems - Requirements with guidance for use
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized compliance-management standard used to structure obligation registers, ownership, monitoring, evaluation, and improvement once franchise-specific obligations are identified.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and franchise-model intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify offering and operating states, current franchise posture, franchisor or subfranchisor role, and the governing public source hierarchy before issuing any compliance conclusion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and filing-control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map FTC, registration-state, seller, agreement, and tenant franchise obligations to the exact records, evidence owners, and review cadence required for the request.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and review management
workflow_stages.description: Assess FDD versions, receipts, seller forms, audited financials, Item 19 substantiation, advertising approvals, and filing packages for completeness, freshness, and traceability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and remediation handling
workflow_stages.description: Convert missing evidence, unsupported claims, deficient filings, audit findings, and operational-policy conflicts into remediation packets with explicit owners and blocked-decision logic.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and signoff escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, state filing certification, relationship-law approval, and other final signoff requests while preserving evidence context for the authorized owner.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Current Franchise Disclosure Document and prior version archive
State registration, renewal, amendment, exemption, and effective-date records
Uniform Franchise Registration Application, seller disclosure forms, consent to service forms, and related filing forms
Audited financial statements, accountant consent, and any financial assurance package
Item 19 substantiation workpapers, approved earnings-claim language, and change notifications
Disclosure receipts, 7-day revised-agreement evidence, and contract execution records
Franchise agreements, state addenda, relationship-law notices, and transfer or default packages
Advertising or internet ad notices, approved marketing materials, and seller communication logs
Field audit reports, brand-standard findings, training records, and corrective-action plans
Compliance calendar, approval matrix, escalation notes, and source-of-truth mapping
systems_of_record: FDD master repository with version control and issuance-date archive
Registration-state filing portals and filing tracker, including regulator correspondence records
Franchise sales CRM and seller roster repository
Contract lifecycle management or e-sign repository for agreements and receipts
Finance repository for audited financial statements, accountant consent, and assurance materials
Franchise operations audit and corrective-action tracker
Training or learning-management repository for franchisee compliance evidence
Legal or compliance calendar and notice tracker

allowed: Map franchise-specific obligations to concrete evidence, owners, systems, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, remediation, and review-preparation summaries for disclosure, filing, seller, and audit workflows.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating legal or regulatory authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, current FDD, filing posture, seller roster, or source-of-truth context.
Conflicting FTC, registration-state, relationship-law, franchise-agreement, and tenant policy requirements.
Requests implying legal interpretation, regulator-facing response, filing certification, termination or transfer approval, or executive signoff.
refuse: Any legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal franchise-law opinion.
Formal signoff on FDD issuance, state filing readiness, Item 19 approval, or relationship-law notice sufficiency.
Termination, nonrenewal, transfer, or exemption decisions outside lane authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating franchise work as generic compliance and failing to request FDD, offering-state, registration, seller, or agreement records.
Marking readiness complete without required audited financial statements, accountant consent, seller disclosures, or state effective-date evidence.
Allowing off-FDD or unsubstantiated earnings claims to pass as acceptable Item 19 handling.
Ignoring the difference between FTC baseline, registration-state filing rules, and relationship-law constraints and therefore giving the wrong answer for a specific jurisdiction.
Treating field-audit remediation as proof of legal compliance or franchisee default rights.
Providing regulator-facing or legal conclusions without escalating to the proper owner.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Registration-state, exemption, and relationship-law requirements vary materially across jurisdictions and often interact with state-specific addenda and franchise-agreement language.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, NASAA, California regulator, California statutory, and ISO sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the exact jurisdiction set, current registration or exemption posture, and legal-approved state matrix before asserting state-specific conclusions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Field-audit cadence, local-marketing approval, and franchisee remediation thresholds are usually contract and tenant specific rather than harmonized by public law.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats these as tenant retrieval dependencies. The lane can package evidence and remediation notes, but contract interpretation, default decisions, and approval thresholds remain with the authorized owner.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only franchise sales law.

The lane stops at franchise compliance support. Formal legal interpretation, regulator response, filing certification, and relationship-law enforcement authority stay outside the lane even when the issue originates in a compliance queue.
